meaning: n.(政治)阴谋小集团,(尤指政治上的)阴谋vi.策划阴谋
examples: -if not for a liberal media cabal systematically lying to people all day.
If there ever was an argument for overcoming cabal resistance to disclosure, surely this is it.
The world’s most conservative scientific body has somehow been transformed into a cabal of screaming demagogues.
The interpretation often requires a cabal of translators, adaptors, actors and a specialist dubbing director.
She retired from her organization, and me from mine, way ahead of the cabal of naysayers.
